summary: Frontend review of PR #53 (InstancedBricks.tsx, FR-PERF-001) is complete. Implementation is APPROVED WITH MINOR NOTES — all critical correctness checks pass. Two non-blocking minor notes: (1) redundant zero-scale loop that can be removed since mesh.count already controls rendering, (2) MAX_INSTANCES should be module-level constant. No blocking issues. CI has not run yet — human must trigger CI and validate FPS performance tests (T-FE-PERF-001-02, T-PERF-PERF-001-01) in a real browser before merging PR #53.

human_review_required.reason: No CI check runs have executed on PR #53. FPS performance tests (T-FE-PERF-001-02: 100 bricks ≥60 FPS, T-PERF-PERF-001-01: 500 bricks ≥30 FPS) require real browser execution. CI runners may not achieve these thresholds headlessly.
Agent review is APPROVED WITH MINOR NOTES. Human must confirm CI passes and FPS targets are met before merging.
mesh.count = bricks.length already prevents rendering of unused instances. The zero-scale loop for i >= bricks.length is redundant and causes unnecessary GPU buffer uploads. Consider removing.
MAX_INSTANCES = 1000 is declared inside the component function body and re-evaluated on every render. Should be moved to module scope.

recommended_actions: Trigger CI on PR #53 (feature/15-instanced-mesh-rendering) to run all unit and e2e tests.
Validate FPS performance tests in a real browser: T-FE-PERF-001-02 (100 bricks ≥60 FPS) and T-PERF-PERF-001-01 (500 bricks ≥30 FPS).
Consider removing the redundant zero-scale loop in InstancedBrickType (non-blocking minor note).
Consider moving MAX_INSTANCES to module scope (non-blocking minor note).
Merge PR #52 (tests) and PR #53 (implementation) once CI passes — PR #52 should merge first or simultaneously.
Close Issue #15 after both PRs are merged.
